entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest Day 2023!!!
entry.content: And as we are almost done with week one of Devtoberfest and this year’s petoberfest I am giving you a goodbye post from JJ, not a genius this guy…

entry.title: 🟡 How Did I Miss That?
entry.content:
As a follow up to the popular session from @MichelleMoudy  last year's "How did I not see that?" We will have another great session for #FunFriday where the Developer Advocates come together to share stories of times where we were involved in a project, a go-live, a push to production, anything that really had an impact on others and we made a mistake and learned from that mistake. 

It's all part of growing and we want to have fun sharing what it is that made us who we are.  Please join us in the chat and comment, ask questions and even share your own stories if you would like.

entry.title: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night Discussion
entry.content:
Thanks so much for attending game night and/or commenting about your favorite games.  This post is now locked from further replies. 

 

Hey everyone!  

If you are attending our game night session 1 or session 2   or if you don't attend, we wanted to provide a place besides the discord server chat where you could come and leave a comment about your experience.  What game did you play?  Did you host a game?  What was your favorite game to as a kid?  Did you have a favorite gaming console?  

Basically if you want some points for the Devtoberfest contest commenting on this thread is a super easy way to get credit for week 2 #FunFriday!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Gaming Night Discussion
entry.content: I wanted to post that my favorite game but also one that drive me nuts was the original Legend of Zelda in the gold cartridge for Nintendo Entertainment System.  It had a battery to save game progress.  I made a deal with myself that once I beat the game I would destroy the cartridge so I wouldn't have to hear that song anymore or be sucked into playing that game again.  But still, my favorite...

entry.title: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content:
Thanks for participating in this Devtoberfest 2023, week 3 activity.  No new responses are being allowed now so we can tally the points earned.

 

So many great ways to earn points this year.  And now in addition to some fan favorites like Game Night and ##Petoberfest we are launching this special week 3 item:  Foodtoberfest for 500 points!

So this group is a place for you to post a picture, a recipe, a comment, anything you want on the items of food you love.  Something you snack on while coding (energy drinks and bananas come to mind).  Or maybe a family favorite you like to cook, mine is Shakshuka. (pic here, but I will post the recipe in the comments below.  Can't wait to see what everyone else post to earn their extra 500 points in the contest!

shakshuka with feta and eggs

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Devtoberfest 2023 Foodtoberfest!!!
entry.content:
• Here is a video I made of me making shakshuka.  Shakshuka is a Northern African/Middle Eastern dish consisting of tomatoes, peppers, onions and spices. A few other items can be changed and woven into the dish.
  • Here are the ingredients and recipe:
  • 3 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil (I used Spanish)
  • 1 large onion or 2 medium onions, halved and thinly sliced
  • 1 large red bell pepper, remove stem and seeds and slice same as onion
  • 3 garlic cloves, sliced
  • 1 teaspoon ground cumin
  • 1 teaspoon paprika (not smoked, regular or sweet)
  • ⅛ teaspoon ground cayenne (red pepper)
  • 1 (28-ounce) can whole san Marzano plum tomatoes, coarsely chopped, keeping liquid from can as well
  • kosher salt (as needed, at least 1.5 teaspoons)
  • black pepper (as needed, at least 1 teaspoon)
  • 5 ounces plain crumbled feta cheese
  • 6 large eggs
  • Chopped cilantro and hot sauce for garnish (both optional)
  • Bread for dipping (like pita or naan)
  • Step 1 Heat oil in a large skillet (cast iron or anything that can go into oven later, over medium-low.
  • Step 2 Chop onion and bell pepper and add to pan. Add pinch salt and pepper to taste. Cook gently until very soft, about 20 minutes.
  • Step 3 While peppers cooking slice garlic thin and chop tomatoes into chunks.
  • Step 4. Add garlic at end of 20 minutes for about 2 additional minutes or until fragrant.
  • Step 5 Add cumin, paprika and cayenne, and cook 1 minute until all mixed into the onion/pepper/garlic mixture.
  • Step 6 heat oven to 375 Fahrenheit
  • Step 7 Pour in tomatoes and season with ¾ teaspoon salt and ¼ teaspoon pepper; taste and see if more seasoning needed. Be aware of spice from cayenne when tasting. Simmer on low to medium heat, it should be bubbling but not boiling until tomatoes have thickened, about 10 minutes. Taste and add more salt and pepper if needed. (If waiting to serve later you can stop here and even keep mixture a day or two before continuing to step 8, just reheat on stovetop and continue. Spices will actually be stronger if you do this).
  • Step 8 Stir in crumbled feta.
  • Step 9 Crack eggs into small bowl and then place into a small depression (divot) in tomatoes mixture but not touching bottom of pan. Repeat until all 6 eggs in pan, . season eggs with salt and pepper lightly. (as I mention in video, you can add meat, tofu, anything pre-cooked you want at this step instead of eggs)
  • Step 10 Transfer pan to oven and bake until eggs are just set, 8 to 10 minutes.
  • Optional Sprinkle with cilantro and serve with hot sauce. Serve with pita/naan bread if you like.

entry.title: 🟡 Fun Friday Trivia - Session 1 of 2
entry.content:
It's trivia time! 🧐

Join us for a fun quiz with questions on SAP and related topics. How will you do? How's your SAP history? Find out, and be ready to join the quiz, which we'll run twice on Friday 13 October.  One winner from each quiz will receive 500 points in the Devtoberfest contest.  To easily award points please use your SAP Community ID as your online trivia name during the session, and that ensures we can see the leader on the quiz software leaderboard in realtime:

- between 1400 and 1500 IST (this instance)

- between 1400 and 1500 EDT (the other instance)

While you'd be more than welcome to join both, the idea is that we can cover as many folks around the world as possible with these two instances. It's just a bit of fun, so if you join the second instance knowing the answers from having joined the first, you're only cheating yourself 🙂

entry.title: 🟡 Fun Friday Trivia - Session 2 of 2
entry.content:
It's trivia time! 🧐

Join us for a fun quiz with questions on SAP and related topics. How will you do? How's your SAP history? Find out, and be ready to join the quiz, which we'll run twice on Friday 13 October.  One winner from each quiz will receive 500 points in the Devtoberfest contest.  To easily award points please use your SAP Community ID as your online trivia name during the session, and that ensures we can see the leader on the quiz software leaderboard in realtime::

- between 1400 and 1500 IST (this other instance)

- between 1400 and 1500 EDT (this instance)

While you'd be more than welcome to join both, the idea is that we can cover as many folks around the world as possible with these two instances. It's just a bit of fun, so if you join the second instance knowing the answers from having joined the first, you're only cheating yourself 🙂 

 

join the quiz at 2PM EDT on youtube to watch the advocates, and the menti website to compete:  https://www.menti.com/aluj469byiby

entry.title: Re: Famtoberfest - this year as part of the Scavenger Hunt!
entry.content: Such a great pic!

entry.title: Re: Desktoberfest - this year as part of the Scavenger Hunt!
entry.content: im getting a theme with your pics

entry.title: São Paulo - SAP Stammtisch 8.8.2024 18:00 BRT #SAPCodeJamsLATAM
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in São Paulo has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

SAP Stammtisch in São Paulo will happen on August 8th, 2024 at 18:00 BRT for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", we have a table reservation for 12 at the Tatu Bola Bar.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates Latin America Roadshow  

and we also invite you join the São Paulo Group if you are not yet the member:  SAP São Paulo

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇱 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Santiago, Chile)
entry.content: Hi @Srimathi if you are with us tomorrow on 13th of August in Santiago Chile we can walk you through all the SAP CodeJam content in person as these sessions are not recorded.  If not local, then please request a local CodeJam on this page.  For other online learning please search SAP Build on our Tutorial and Learning Journey sites.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: This cat lives in my house, I do not claim him or offer as much attention as he would like. We tolerate each other...

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Spooktoberfest 2024
entry.content: this guy was very sad when I mentioned I wanted a pic for end of this year’s Devtoberfest. I told him we will be back next year. Then we sang Putting on the Ritz.

entry.title: 4 updates for you from Mr. Bluebeard about Sapphire, Community and a Learning podcast.
entry.content:
My ride's coming, so I got to go. Hey, everybody. Mr. Bluebeard, Josh Bentley, and I know I need to touch out the blue a little bit, but I'll get that done this weekend, and the reason is I've got four updates in this video. 

The first today is I'm going to be doing my Bluebeard much bluer this weekend as I head down to Sapphire Orlando, so I'll be there next Monday through Wednesday. If you can't be on site in Orlando, then you can be online watching different sessions. I will be doing a Wednesday session on the power of the SAP community. I'm going to be joined by Anita Riegel and Danielle Larocca. Danielle is one of our SAP mentors, and she's going to share about her experience and all the great things that she does as a member of ASUG, as well as as a mentor, and how she uses the SAP community. So watch that remotely, or if you're on site, please join us. and then going to be meeting with lots of mentors and champions on site and having a great time doing that and just those side conversations that are really powerful when you get to meet with other people.

Second update is that I'm kind of selfish, right? And the reason I'm selfish is I'm not just going to Sapphire to learn about all the great content we talk about. It's to see how you communicate with us about it in the SAP community. So if you're watching my video here on LinkedIn or if you're on the SAP community, tag me, ask questions after Sapphire. You can also tag other blog posts that I'm not in with my name, if it's on the SAP community or on LinkedIn or wherever you are, YouTube, and just say, hey, I have a question about whatever technology was talked about at Sapphire. So I'll do my best to get an answer for you or to connect you with the right person. That's why I want to express that I'm selfish because I want you in the community to share with others. And do that where you are. And if you can, bring us into the conversation to help facilitate where needed.

Third update is that Ann Petteroe published a great blog post describing some changes that were recently made to the navigation menu of how we're organizing our coffee corner section and just some of the areas where people are posting with their interest. So read Anne's blog post that I'll put in the description of this video and just see what we're doing to try to help the navigation of the SAP community site be a little better. Always looking for your feedback, and I know that you guys are more than willing to give us that to make your community as good as it can be.

And then the fourth update is that there's a link in here as well to the podcast from the SAP News team. The News Education team does a regular podcast in German and another one in English. And I was on their English -speaking podcast. Almost hour-long conversation, getting to know each other and talk about SAP learning and the community and everything that we work on together. And then finally, I need to get done with this video so I can get picked up from my ride. As you can see here, I'm standing in front of the helipad and my ride's coming. so i gotta go that's how i should be getting to orlando right from a look no anyway i look forward to seeing you guys there next week talk to you later from the sapphire show floor bye

 

Sapphire upate:

https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/virtual.html?campaigncode=crm-yd25-crs-3141407&source=ppc-glo-googleads-pmax-22504838353--events_x-x-x-x&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=22494796053&gbraid=0AAAAAoTWXrBDPwih6eBuuibBTO1BLlaEe&gclid=CjwKCAjwuIbBBhBvEiwAsNypvaw2YDHcpx02ggBqDcW058EfquTx3YGyluPYFORvtTxl-H7_EWM3ORoCBj4QAvD_BwE&gclsrc=aw.ds

Tag me here with comments or on our YouTube Channels:

SAP Community - https://www.youtube.com/SAPCommunities

SAP Developers - https://www.youtube.com/@sapdevs

Anne’s post about site navigation updates that aren’t just welcoming:

https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/new-blog-post-board-in-the-community-coffee-corner-group/ba-p/14097427

SAP Learning News Podcast:

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/thomasjenewein_sapcommunity-community-activity-7327584765874745344-Gcji?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAADZyOIBBnJ1Ez8yjotmlgYEabufoq3f7EI

Introduction and Update to SAP Community with Josh Bentley

🎧SAPPodcasts https://lnkd.in/ePZdSDma

🎧Apple https://lnkd.in/eUXGmeEq

🎧Spotify https://lnkd.in/dZQ75tU

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content: Yes @moshenaveh I will be at this year's #Sapphire.  Here are a couple of my event memories from my first SAP TechEd's international I wanted to share.  My first trip to India and Spain!  So many people in the Developer Garage and so many great team members needed to pull off such an amazing event,  I see so many faces I am not sure I can tag them all @kimmecca @Ian_Thain @Dan_Wroblewski @craigcmehil @sveabecker @rileyrainey @qmacro  and so many more... Developer Garage Bengaluru 2018Barcelona SAP TechEd team
